add-new-agent-provider.blurb: Integrating a new coding-agent CLI into regin's capture pipeline
add-new-agent-provider.intent: Show a future agent the exact contract and integration points for teaching regin about a new coding-agent CLI (Kimi/Codex-style): subclass the AgentProvider adapter, register it, declare its capabilities/format flags, and wire path resolution, transcript parsing, hook install, permission shaping, and skill deployment so regin captures the agent's sessions without hard-coding vendor paths or payload shapes across the shared pipeline.
add-new-agent-provider.label: Adding a new agent provider (AgentProvider adapter playbook: contract, registry, hook/transcript wiring)

agent-memory.blurb: Capture / distil / store / recall / reflect / feedback / exemplars of cross-session memory — enter for tasks touching memory writes, recall, or lessons. Code: lib/memory/ (except evaluate.py)
agent-memory.intent: Capture / distil / store / recall / reflect / feedback / exemplars of cross-session memory — enter for tasks touching memory writes, recall, or lessons. Code: lib/memory/ (except evaluate.py)
agent-memory.label: Agent memory engine

agent-memory-architecture.blurb: Start here before touching memory: the facade, ports/adapters seam, scoping, the self-initializing DB, and the tables.
agent-memory-architecture.intent: Orient a future agent in regin's cross-session memory subsystem before touching any of it. `__init__.py` is a thin module-verb facade delegating to a process-wide `SqliteMemoryStore` built with a `SkillRouterEmbedding`. `ports.py` declares the four decoupling interfaces (`EmbeddingProvider`, `LLMProvider`, `MemoryStore`, `MemorySink`), each degrading gracefully; concrete providers live in `adapters.py` at the edge (`SkillRouterEmbedding` with its optional `embed_queries`/`rerank` extensions, `ExternalAgentLLM` over a configured external command, and the `resolve_distiller`/`resolve_topic_classifier`/`resolve_proposal_reviewer` surface binders). `scoping.py` stamps `global`/`repo:<name>`. The single mutable `memories` table carries both tiers via a `tier` column; `models.py` declares its own `memory_metadata` so `regin init`/`rebuild` and Alembic never touch memory tables, and `engine.py` self-initializes a third engine on `db/regin_memory.db`. `mcp_server.py` is the long-lived MCP entrypoint.
agent-memory-architecture.label: Agent memory architecture (ports/adapters engine, self-initializing DB, tiers & lifecycle model)

aliases: supersede a progress message by key
telegram lark and webhook push notifications
what events can reach my inbox
agent-messages-inbox.blurb: Changing how inbox messages are produced, stored, or shown — send_to_user progress messages, the declared notification event bus (proposals / drift / grades / permission / plan), push fan-out, or the Inbox UI.
agent-messages-inbox.intent: How an inbox message reaches the human. Two producers feed one store: (1) agent-authored `send_to_user` MCP calls — the session-blind stdio server only declares the schema, and the PostToolUse hook (which alone knows the session) persists them through the single `store.record_message` writer; (2) a declared notification event bus (`events.py`) over which every non-`send_to_user` system event — an agent blocked on a permission/plan, an external proposal drafted, wiki content drift, a session grade landing, a topic proposed for suppression — is declared once in `REGISTRY` and published through one `emit`, routing to the same writer with a deep-link URL. High-severity types fan out to three pluggable push channels (webhook / Telegram / Lark-Feishu); the Flask API + Vue Inbox render the per-session feed and cross-session unread badge; `regin events list` / `GET /api/events/kinds` enumerate the bus.
agent-messages-inbox.label: Inbox messages & the notification event bus (send_to_user inbox message store, cross-session Inbox, declared event design, push fan-out)

agent-providers.label: Agent provider adapters

data-config.intent: ORM / SQLModel, db/schema.sql, settings, auth, multi-user, sync — enter when touching schema, settings, auth, or sync. Code: lib/orm/, lib/settings.py, lib/auth.py, lib/sync/

aliases: session has fewer spans than the transcript
debug-missing-trace-span.blurb: A session shows fewer spans than its transcript holds — triage the hooks log, ingest-errors, and seen-uuid cache, then run the repair.
commands: sqlite3 ~/.local/share/regin/regin.db "SELECT name, count(*) FROM session_spans WHERE trace_id='<trace_id>' GROUP BY name ORDER BY name"
.venv/bin/python -c "from lib.trace.repair import repair_session_spans; import json; print(json.dumps(repair_session_spans('<trace_id>'), indent=2))"
debug-missing-trace-span.intent: Step-by-step procedure for diagnosing why a Claude Code (or Codex / generic-provider) session has fewer rows in regin's session_spans table than the on-disk JSONL transcript implies, and for driving recovery. Triage starts in the hook activity log (feature=hooks) to confirm the turn_trace handler fired, then walks the ingest-errors log, the JSONL transcript, and the per-session seen-uuid cache. The recovery path is lib.trace.repair.repair_session_spans (also POST /api/sessions/<trace_id>/repair-spans), which trims the locked-out uuids, replays turn_trace's live emit pipeline, and materializes the healed projection. Verification reads back from session_spans and confirms the span_repair_completed activity-log row. Every step is shell + the regin venv.
debug-missing-trace-span.label: Debug a missing trace span (hooks-log triage, ingest-errors, seen-uuid cache lockout, repair endpoint)

Code: lib/grader/, lib/memory/evaluate.py, lib/experiments.py, lib/goal_feedback.py
eval-grading.intent: LLM-judge session grading (rubric / correctness / coverage / grounding / agentic), recall-quality eval, pattern-conceal experiments, goal verification — enter for anything that measures quality or judges good/bad. eval-grading.label: Evaluation & grading

aliases: pin the bar before the agent builds
hard-gate floor plus opt-in recalled lessons
reinforce included lessons, decay dropped ones
close the build loop back into agent memory
file a failure lesson under a topic node
goal-verified-loop.blurb: Wiring `regin goal preflight`/`feedback`, the hard-gate floor, or how a goal run pins the bar and feeds memory.
goal-verified-loop.intent: The two regin-side halves of the `/goal-verified` loop-engineering workflow (the build + verify halves are the skill). Preflight is a deterministic, offline kernel: it assembles a roadmap of the universal hard-gate floor (tests stay green + an independent fresh-context reviewer) plus, opt-in, a short menu of FTS-recalled past lessons — pinning a falsifiable bar before code is written. Per-area convention skills and reference components are sourced outside this kernel (the file-keyed table in CLAUDE.local.md and the real target files). Feedback closes the loop after verification, reusing memory primitives only: included lessons are reinforced, FAILED acceptance items become area-tagged `goal-verified-fail` lesson memories, and those failure-lessons are filed under agent-supplied authoritative topic nodes.
goal-verified-loop.label: Loop-engineering: goal preflight roadmap → verified build → feedback into memory

hooks-injection.blurb: hook_plugin, UserPromptSubmit / PostToolUse wiring, prompt-template injection — enter when tuning hook behaviour or injected content. Code: lib/hook_plugin.py, lib/prompt_templates.py
hooks-injection.intent: hook_plugin, UserPromptSubmit / PostToolUse wiring, prompt-template injection — enter when tuning hook behaviour or injected content. Code: lib/hook_plugin.py, lib/prompt_templates.py
hooks-injection.label: Hooks & injection

aliases: steer the agent from the phone
answer an ask-user question on mobile
server phase verdict for the live card
scope the tail to one subagent
live-session-mobile-card.blurb: Building or debugging the /live phone card — its poll cadence, row/qa filtering, server-phase NOW-zone states, bridge composer, agent scoping, or session switcher — start here.
live-session-mobile-card.intent: Explains the /live/:id? mobile-first single-card session view: its header/tail/NOW-zone/scope-bar shape and bottom-sheet interactions, the useLiveTail composable that pages/polls/reconciles the shallow trace map, the liveRows.js row-categorization / signal-tier / human-phrasing rules including the qa and phase-band rows, the server-authoritative phase model that both the header and the LiveNowZone render (never re-derive), the bridge composer + answerable Q&A sheet + raw-terminal peek + slash-command autocomplete, per-agent scoping and roster, and the recent-sessions switcher — for anyone building, extending, or debugging the mobile live view.
live-session-mobile-card.label: /live mobile session-tail card (mobile-first live session view, poll/reconcile composable, server-phase NOW zone, bridge composer)

aliases: how past lessons reach the prompt
memory-auto-injection.blurb: How lessons reach the prompt: the UserPromptSubmit hook, the loopback recall borrow, and the long-lived recall/tree-nav MCP server.
memory-auto-injection.intent: Show how recalled memories reach an agent and why the delivery path is shaped the way it is. The `UserPromptSubmit` handler is a fresh short-lived process per prompt, so it borrows the warm `regin serve` embedder via a loopback-auth-exempt POST to `/api/memory/recall` (`_recall_via_server`) and falls back to in-process FTS-only (`_recall_fts`); it routes the prompt to an authoritative topic and can withhold a `<topic_context>` banner. Speculative injection never reinforces — overlap-gated, uncalibrated-capped, same-session deduped via `injection_events` — except the earned `reinforce_resurfaced`; each inject and routed banner is recorded and `_emit_recall_span` writes a `memory.recall` span. `api_memory_recall` computes route-time topic suppression (it needs the warm embedder) and returns hits plus `topic_suppress`. The long-lived `recall` MCP server exposes `recall`, the address-only tree-nav tools `index_root`/`index_expand`/`index_fetch`, and a `gate` tool for trace-derived span gates.
memory-auto-injection.label: Memory auto-injection & on-demand recall (UserPromptSubmit hook, recall MCP server, /api/memory/recall)

aliases: the sleep cycle of agent memory
attach synthesis cluster to topic graph
memory-consolidation-reflect.blurb: The offline sleep cycle: how working rows are deduped, promoted, decayed, synthesized, and retired.
memory-consolidation-reflect.intent: Explain `reflect()` — the batch consolidation cycle that turns raw `working` rows into curated `episodic` knowledge — step by ordered step: near-duplicate dedup (`_dedup_and_judge`, embedding cosine or text-ratio fallback), LLM-judged contradiction in the similarity gray zone, working→episodic promotion with `recall_count`-driven importance (`_promote`), forget-stale retirement of never-recalled aged rows (`_forget_stale`), a pre-decay engagement densifying sweep (`_score_pending`), importance decay for chronically-ignored rows whose gate reads the soft/hard engagement split (`_decay_chronically_ignored`/`_decay_reason`), stale-reference flagging with git rename-follow (`_flag_stale_references`), LLM synthesis of higher-order rules from cosine clusters (`_synthesize`), an optional per-scope digest (`_synthesize_digest`), content-hash-skipped embedding (`_embed_episodic`), and the `related` edge harvest (`_harvest_edges`). `topic_attach.py` proposes merge/create onto the human-approved `.regin/topics` graph.
memory-consolidation-reflect.label: Memory consolidation (reflect: dedup, contradiction, promote, synthesize, forget-stale, decay, edges & topics)

aliases: file a memory under a topic node
memory-curation-surfaces.blurb: Curate the memory store by hand: approve/retire/restore/forget memories, run reflect, check store health, file memories under topics, walk the taxonomy tree, stamp topic-route exemplars, decide topic suppression, share a memory tree over git, and probe recall.
memory-curation-surfaces.intent: Point an agent at the human-facing controls for curating the cross-session memory store — the curation that is the whole point of memory being mutable. Covers the `/api/memory/*` Flask blueprint (`web/blueprints/memory.py`), the four-tab `MemoryView.vue` dashboard with its `frontend/src/components/memory/*` panels (including the `MemoryDoctor` health panel), and the `regin memory` Typer CLI (`cli/commands/memory.py`). These are the three coordinated entry points that wrap the `lib.memory` facade for reviewing distill proposals, fixing a memory's veracity, filing memories under topic nodes, walking the topic taxonomy, hand-curating signed topic-route exemplars, deciding which topic banners get withheld, sharing a memory tree over git, and probing what a query would surface.
memory-curation-surfaces.label: Memory curation surfaces (Memory view, /api/memory/* blueprint, regin memory CLI)

aliases: implicit capture from a finished session
memory-distillation-capture.blurb: Where memories come from: the explicit lesson tee and the agentic post-session distiller with its importance gate.
memory-distillation-capture.intent: Explain the two ways a memory is born and why distillation is LLM-only by contract: the explicit path (`send_to_user(type=lesson)` teed into the store by `post_tool_trace._remember_lesson` with span/agent/scope provenance, honoring a `supersedes` id), and the implicit agentic distiller (`distill.py::distill_session`) that reads a finished session's trace, is handed only the high-signal hints (`_grade_digest` grader findings + `_signal_digest` failure→fix / pushback chains) plus read-only trace tools and self-fetches just the spans it needs so prompt size stays constant. Covers the self-scored importance band (`_finalize_status`: drop below `distill_min_importance`, auto-approve at/above `auto_approve_importance`, else queue as `proposed`), dedup-at-write reinforcement (`_dedup_candidate`), at-write contradiction supersede (`_supersede_candidate`, gated by `distill_supersede_on_conflict`), meta-root auto-filing (`_link_meta_root`), and the `DISTILL_TAG` idempotency guard.
memory-distillation-capture.label: Memory capture & distillation (send_to_user lesson tee + agentic post-session distiller)

aliases: did the injected memory actually help
referent appears in a later span
soft vs hard ignore decay signal
memory-engagement-feedback.blurb: The did-it-help verdict: referents in later spans, idf-weighted, stamped onto injection events for the decay gate.
memory-engagement-feedback.intent: Explain the half of the recall loop that learns whether a speculatively-injected memory helped: `score_injection_usefulness` pulls concrete referents (file paths, backtick identifiers) out of each injected memory and checks whether any appear in a tool span that fired *after* injection (the ordering gate is load-bearing), abstaining when there are no referents. Covers the idf weighting that discounts corpus-ubiquitous referents (precomputed session document-frequency in `referent_session_df` via `rebuild_session_referent_df`, gated by `engagement_idf_min_weight`), the `engaged`/`matched` bits stamped once per `injection_events` row so the decay gate can tell a soft ignore from a hard one, the importance reward for engaged hits, the reflect-time `score_pending_sessions` densifying sweep, and how this positive signal pairs with reflect's negative decay half.
memory-engagement-feedback.label: Memory engagement feedback (inject→usefulness verdict, idf-weighted referents, decay gate coupling)

aliases: why is this topic route suppressed for some prompts
thumbs up down on a routed topic banner
memory-exemplar-rescore.blurb: Query-local signed exemplars that suppress or protect a routed topic banner for prompts like the ones it failed/passed on.
memory-exemplar-rescore.intent: Explain regin's signed query-exemplar mechanism as it operates over authoritative-topic routing — the contextual, query-local complement to the global, slow `importance` decay. An exemplar is the embedding of a prompt a topic banner was injected on, tagged `polarity` +1 (protect) or -1 (suppress) and `source` `auto`/`manual`; `topic_exemplars` keeps them per (topic, polarity), each polarity capped. At route time `store.topic_route_suppressed` withholds a banner query-locally when the prompt's cosine to the topic's negatives clears `topic_negative_suppress_sim` unless a positive protects it or a human `allowed` pin overrides. Covers the auto write path (`apply_topic_relevance`/`_record_topic_exemplars` → `add_topic_negatives`/`add_topic_positives`), the manual curate path (`/api/memory/exemplars`, `ExemplarCaseList.vue`, `regin memory exemplar-*`), the route-signal probe (`topic_query_signals`), and the inspect/per-row revert surfaces. The route-decision half is `memory-topic-route-feedback`; the design note is `docs/agent-memory-exemplars.md`.
memory-exemplar-rescore.label: Signed query exemplars — topic-route suppression/protection (auto & hand-curated)

aliases: how a memory is scored and ranked
hybrid retrieval over the memory corpus
memory-recall-pipeline.blurb: Tuning or debugging why a memory ranks where it does: the FTS+dense+rerank+quality+MMR read stack.
memory-recall-pipeline.intent: Explain the read-side ranking stack a `recall()` request flows through in `store.py`, so an agent debugging or tuning recall knows which knob moves which result. `recall` embeds the query once, fuses `_lexical_ids` (FTS5/BM25) and `_dense_ids` (cosine over `memory_embeddings`, with `_lazy_backfill`) via `_rrf`, narrows to `_eligible_rows`, applies the `_gate_lexical_overlap` precision gate (idf-filtered informative tokens), reranks the RRF head with the cross-encoder (`_order_candidates`, sigmoid confidence, `rerank_cap`), multiplies by the bounded `_quality_factor`, softly lifts topic-linked rows (`_apply_topic_boost`), applies optional MMR diversity (`_mmr_select`) and 1-hop edge expansion (`_expand_via_edges`), and reinforces on deliberate recall (`_bump_recall`). `expand.py` is the LLM query rewrite; `evaluate.py` the FTS-only regression harness.
memory-recall-pipeline.label: Memory recall pipeline (FTS + dense + RRF + rerank, quality weighting, topic boost, MMR, edge expansion, query expansion)

memory-topic-route-feedback.blurb: Should a routed topic banner keep firing: the InjectedRelated verdict, per-topic fail rate, and the human suppression gate.
memory-topic-route-feedback.intent: Explain the relevance feedback loop wrapped around authoritative-topic routing — the topic analog of memory engagement feedback. The `UserPromptSubmit` hook records each injected `<topic_context>` banner as a `TopicInjection` row; the grader's `InjectedRelated` aspect (`config/settings.json` + `lib/grader/service._maybe_apply_injection_relevance`) judges whether the banner fit the goal and `store.apply_topic_relevance` stamps a `satisfied`|`needs_revision`|`fail` verdict once per session. Aggregated per topic (`topic_relevance_summary`, `topic_relevance_stats`), a recurring fail rate over `topic_relevance_min_scored`/`topic_relevance_fail_rate` only *proposes* suppression — withholding is human-in-the-loop via a `TopicRouteDecision` (`suppressed`/`allowed`/auto) that gates the hook's `_route_topic`. A `fail` also becomes a -1 topic exemplar and a `pass` a +1, so `topic_route_suppressed` can withhold query-locally. The route playground (`topic_query_signals` → `/api/memory/topic-route-preview`, `TopicRoutePlayground.vue`/`MemoryTopicFeedback.vue`) probes what a query would route to and why.
memory-topic-route-feedback.label: Topic-route relevance feedback (InjectedRelated verdict, fail-rate proposal, human suppression gate, route playground)

aliases: how other users see my topics
multi-user-topic-sharing.blurb: Drill in when an approved topic shows on one machine but not a teammate's, or when wiring git-based topic sync across clones.
multi-user-topic-sharing.intent: Explain how regin's standalone-per-user model lets one user's approved topics become visible to other users: what travels via git (topic.json + wiki/<id>.md), what stays local (topic.local.json overlay, snapshot DB, in-flight proposals), and the producer/consumer steps (promote -> pre-commit add -> teammate git pull -> post-merge `topics import` -> snapshot DB seeded -> routable & visible in WebUI). Also covers the shared-server alternative when cross-user proposal review is needed.
multi-user-topic-sharing.label: Multi-user topic sharing (how approved topics travel from one user to another)

ops-cli.label: CLI & ops

pattern-experiments.intent: Understand the experiment system that conceals selected H2/H3 sections of a pattern's SKILL.md at deploy time so rule-trigger rates can be compared against an unconcealed baseline. Covers the pure conceal filter, the experiments table and RuleTrigger.experiment_id linkage, the one-active-per-pattern lifecycle, the redeploy hook into skill_sync/skill_deployer, the baseline-vs-variant rollup endpoints, the Vue UI surfaces, and the gap between the ingest path and the rollup's variant column.
pattern-experiments.label: Pattern Conceal Experiments

patterns-skills.blurb: Local pattern guides + skill deploy/read — enter when writing a pattern, deploying a skill, or editing the skill index. Code: lib/patterns/, lib/skills/
patterns-skills.intent: Local pattern guides + skill deploy/read — enter when writing a pattern, deploying a skill, or editing the skill index. Code: lib/patterns/, lib/skills/
patterns-skills.label: Patterns & skills

proposal-review-comments.blurb: Drill in to work on proposal-draft review threads: anchoring, resolution, regenerate carry-forward, the auto-addressed sweep, or the content-drift 'Not a real drift' dismissal.
proposal-review-comments.intent: Document the inline review-comment system layered on a proposal run: how a feedback thread is anchored (general / topic field / proposal summary / wiki range), the thread kinds (human comment, agent review_note, agent content_drift note), what HTTP endpoints CRUD threads and comments, how the ORM stores them with revision pointers, how open threads are formatted into the next regenerate prompt, how the auto-`addressed` sweep snaps a thread closed when its anchored content changes between revisions (diffing a wiki_range note against the anchored topic's OWN per-topic wiki so a scoped regenerate doesn't close untouched topics' notes), and the 'Not a real drift' escape hatch that re-baselines a topic's ref digests and dismisses its content-drift signal on both the origin-run note and the standalone fallback proposal so it stops re-firing.
proposal-review-comments.label: Proposal review comments (feedback threads anchored to topics & wiki, incl. content-drift notes)

recall-eval-verification.intent: Proving a recall change actually works: verify end-to-end through the real auto-inject path (not units), why silently-degrading fast paths and ungated FTS fallbacks hide breakage, embed-coverage completeness, mechanical probes that must not reinforce, and --json output hygiene. Drill in before trusting a recall eval.
recall-eval-verification.label: Recall eval & end-to-end verification (true inject path, silent-degradation gotchas)

aliases: where regin logs its own operations
tail and grep the activity log
regin-activity-log-observability.blurb: Inspecting or extending regin's own ops history — activity log, structured logging, or the dashboard audit trail
regin-activity-log-observability.intent: Map regin's self-observability stack — distinct from `lib/trace/` session traces. `lib/activity_log.py` writes one rotating `feature`-tagged JSONL stream (read=DEBUG / write=INFO discipline, secret-key auto-redaction), surfaced from the terminal via the `regin logs` group (list / tail / grep / prune / path). `lib/logging_setup.py` bootstraps structlog so Flask/SQLAlchemy/lib all emit into one pipeline with console vs. JSON rendering modes. `lib/audit.py` records who changed what in the web dashboard (best-effort, never blocks, routed through AuthSessionLocal). Use this when diagnosing regin's behaviour, not an agent session.
regin-activity-log-observability.label: Ops observability: activity log, structured logging & audit trail (regin's own operations history vs. session traces)

aliases: how the regin command line is wired
where do CLI command bodies live
regin-cli-architecture.blurb: Adding, grouping, or debugging a `regin` CLI command, or running doctor / init / rebuild
regin-cli-architecture.intent: Explain how the `regin` CLI is assembled and how to extend it: the thin `cli/regin.py` entrypoint that re-execs under the project `.venv` and fixes sys.path, the Typer root in `cli/app.py` that wires per-domain modules under `cli/commands/` (each exposing `register(app)` for flat commands or a pre-built sub-Typer for `regin <group> <cmd>`), the root callback that stamps every invocation into the activity log, and the shared `cli/deps.py` (`require_db` guard) + `cli/output.py` (testable echo/error/table sinks) helpers. `doctor`, `init --force`, and `rebuild --clean` are the canonical ops/lifecycle commands, backed by `lib/doctor.py` and `lib/db_rebuild.py`.
regin-cli-architecture.label: regin CLI architecture & command surface (Typer entrypoint, venv re-exec, per-domain command registry, doctor/db lifecycle, add-a-command playbook)

rule-engine-design.intent: Explain how regin loads, dispatches, and executes lint/rewrite rules: the engine-agnostic Protocol, the three-source registry precedence (explicit settings → legacy grit fallback → autodiscovered bundles), the three concrete adapters (Grit, Bundle, Radon), the PostToolUse hook that drives them per edit, the two in-tree example bundles under example/rule/, and the step-by-step playbook for adding a new engine. Use this when adding a new engine kind, packaging a new rule pack as a bundle, wiring an existing engine into a new language, importing one of the example bundles, or debugging why a rule fired (or didn't) on a file.
rule-engine-design.label: Rule engine design (adapter protocol, registry, PostToolUse driver, in-tree example bundles, add-an-engine playbook)

rule-engines.blurb: GritQL / Radon / Bundle lint engines, language adapters, PostToolUse enforcement — enter when changing a lint rule or engine. Code: lib/rule_engines/, lib/rules/, lib/languages/
rule-engines.intent: GritQL / Radon / Bundle lint engines, language adapters, PostToolUse enforcement — enter when changing a lint rule or engine. Code: lib/rule_engines/, lib/rules/, lib/languages/
rule-engines.label: Rule engines (lint/rewrite)

aliases: get my session id from a bash command
why a child session id differs from the task output path
resolve the live session id for goal feedback
session-id-probe.blurb: Debugging or extending how a Bash command recovers its own Claude Code session id.
session-id-probe.intent: How a Bash command recovers its own Claude Code session id. Claude Code (>= ~2.1) exports the live id into every child process's environment as `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`; `lib/session_probe.py::resolve()` reads it and the always-present `regin session-id` subcommand prints it (empty stdout + exit 0 on a miss, so callers treat blank as 'omit the flag'). For a child session the env var is the child's own id — where that context's trace spans land — so skills read it here rather than reconstructing it from a Task tool's output path. Consumed by `goal preflight --session-id`, `goal feedback --trace-id`, and the `gate` anti-skip checks.
session-id-probe.label: Session-id probe (recovering the live Claude Code session id inside a Bash command)

session-trace.blurb: OTel-style span ingest, aggregates, merge, trace UI data — enter when changing trace capture, span attribution, or aggregation. Code: lib/trace/
session-trace.intent: OTel-style span ingest, aggregates, merge, trace UI data — enter when changing trace capture, span attribution, or aggregation. Code: lib/trace/
session-trace.label: Session trace

session-trace-design.blurb: Changing trace capture, span persistence, session aggregates, or the sessions-list / per-trace dashboard read paths
session-trace-design.intent: Explain how Claude Code sessions are captured as OpenTelemetry-style spans by hook handlers, persisted through the trace_service ingest layer into session_spans plus a denormalized sessions row, reconciled at read time by the serve-time merge, and rendered by the Vue SessionsView list and the composable-driven SessionTraceView per-trace dashboard via the /api/sessions/* read endpoints.
session-trace-design.label: Session trace design (OTel-style spans, ingest pipeline, aggregates, dashboard read paths)

topic-graph.blurb: The topic taxonomy itself: routing, proposals, review, multi-user sharing, wiki — enter when changing topic structure, routing, or proposals. Code: lib/topics/
topic-graph.intent: The topic taxonomy itself: routing, proposals, review, multi-user sharing, wiki — enter when changing topic structure, routing, or proposals. Code: lib/topics/
topic-graph.label: Topic graph & routing

topic-proposal-pipeline.intent: Explain the end-to-end funnel that turns a user's topic request into rows of `.regin/topics/topic.json`: an external tool-using agent drafts per-topic JSON + wiki, regin validates and persists it ORM-first through the proposal ORM, the agent self-ingests via a notify-on-finish command in its own process (capturing its real drafting-session trace id and firing an inbox `proposal.ready` card), reviewers iterate via regenerate/restore/feedback/review-state (with scoped content-drift regenerate that splices only drifted topics and keeps untouched wikis byte-identical), and a single server-side /diff + /apply path (or the legacy accept/replace/merge/ignore helpers) promotes a topic into the approved graph. Also covers the two orthogonal status axes (run lifecycle vs review state), the agentic review-note reviewer, the cross-thread cancel machinery that lets Stop terminate the agent subprocess, and the reaper that fails runs orphaned by a serve restart.
topic-proposal-pipeline.label: Topic proposal pipeline (request → draft → review → apply/stop)

aliases: resolve a query into topic context
topic-routing.blurb: How a query becomes approved topic context: the matcher, keyword scoring, the route envelope, the --wiki output, and the topic-router skill.
topic-routing.intent: Understand how a freeform query resolves to approved topic context. Covers the priority-ordered matcher in lib/topics/route.py (alias/label exact -> ref-path exact -> ref-path substring -> label/intent substring, then a fuzzy multi-keyword fallback), the keyword-signal model (a wordfreq English-rarity prior in route.py scaled by the repo-adaptive query-log document-frequency multiplier in lib/topics/term_weights.py, plus a per-graph saturation filter that drops corpus-common terms), the route_topic envelope that returns ordered refs + bounded wiki pages + first-degree related topics with an approved/unmatched status, the best_topic_for_text reverse signal for long text, the generated topic-router skill, and the regin topics route/route --wiki/router-skill/rebuild-query-df CLI commands.
topic-routing.label: Topic routing (keyword router over the approved graph + topic-router skill)

trace-merge-reconcile.intent: The READ-time assembly path in merge.py: serve-time merge_spans, lookahead reattachment of UserPromptSubmit spans, dropped/discarded vs deleted render states, count-vs-items divergence, cancelled-tool span loss, segment-aware ctx%, and prompt-edit forks vs real /rewinds. Drill in when spans exist but render wrong.
trace-merge-reconcile.label: Trace serve-time merge & reconcile (reparent, reattach, forks/rewinds, render states)

trace-span-capture.intent: Adding or reshaping a span TYPE: a new _TOOL_BUILDERS entry, its payload schema, the attr-whitelist for an injected block, wiring a new traced system-event subtype across the capture layers, or normalizing a non-Claude provider's transcript (Kimi) into spans. Drill in when the question is 'how do I make X show up as a span'.
trace-span-capture.label: Trace span capture (builders, payload schemas, new system subtypes, provider quirks)

trace-usage-billing.intent: Token and dollar accounting for a session: turn_usage rows, why a Task-subagent's API spend goes missing, Workflow billing $0 from a cache-less rate shard, subagent transcript nesting depth, and gating the seen-cache on ingest success. Drill in when the bill or token rollup looks wrong.
trace-usage-billing.label: Trace usage & billing (turn_usage, token/cost accounting, subagent bill attribution)

unclassified.blurb: Topics awaiting a human bucket assignment. Appearing here is a curation backlog item to place under a real bucket.
unclassified.intent: Topics awaiting a human bucket assignment. Appearing here is a curation backlog item to place under a real bucket.
unclassified.label: Unclassified

webui.blurb: All Vue 3 SPA views + Flask /api blueprints — enter when changing a frontend page or its API. Code: frontend/, web/
webui.intent: All Vue 3 SPA views + Flask /api blueprints — enter when changing a frontend page or its API. Code: frontend/, web/
webui.label: Web UI & API

webui-dev-loop.intent: Environment/iteration mechanics around working on the UI, not the UI itself: restart the vite dev server after vite.config proxy changes, tmux panes not inheriting the spawner's PATH (absolutise argv[0]), and send_to_user links being file paths not URLs. Drill in when the dev loop misbehaves rather than the rendered surface.
webui-dev-loop.label: WebUI dev-loop mechanics (vite proxy restart, tmux PATH, send_to_user link form)

webui-styling.intent: CSS/render-level fixes in the Vue SPA: scoped styles not crossing into extracted child components (use global classes), data-theme palette inversion, Reka/Radix portalled content escaping scoped styles, the .input base class vs Tailwind utilities, focus-ring-as-containment, the Icon primitive (no emoji/inline svg), and measuring table-fixed cell overflow by text Range not the td box.
webui-styling.label: WebUI styling & layout gotchas (scoped CSS reach, theming, portalled content, table overflow)

aliases: what screens does the dashboard have
where is the view for this route
webui-surface.blurb: Which Vue view owns a route, how the sidebar is grouped, and how Flask serves the SPA.
webui-surface.intent: Give a future agent a one-stop index of every page in the regin dashboard: which Vue view renders each route, how the routes are grouped in the sidebar, which feature flags hide a group, and how the SPA is built and served by Flask. It is a navigational map that hands off to the deep feature topics (memory, trace, rules, topics) rather than re-explaining them.
webui-surface.label: WebUI surface map (Vue 3 SPA pages, router routes, sidebar nav groups & the Flask shell that serves them)
